segments.text: And Jacob called his sons and said,
Gather together, that I may tell you what shall befall you in the last days:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Gather together and hear, you sons of Jacob, And listen to Israel your father.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Reuben, you are my firstborn, My might and the beginning of my strength, The excellency of dignity and the excellency of power.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Unstable as water, you shall not excel, Because you went up to your father's bed; Then you defiled it—He went up to my couch.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Simeon and Levi are brothers; Instruments of cruelty are in their dwelling place.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Let not my soul enter their council; Let not my honor be united to their assembly; For in their anger they slew a man, And in their self-will they hamstrung an ox.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce; And their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob And scatter them in Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Judah, you are he whom your brothers shall praise; Your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; Your father's children shall bow down before you.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Judah is a lion's whelp; From the prey, my son, you have gone up. He bows down, he lies down as a lion; And as a lion, who shall rouse him?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, Nor a lawgiver from between his feet, Until Shiloh comes; And to Him shall be the obedience of the people.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Binding his donkey to the vine, And his donkey's colt to the choice vine, He washed his garments in wine, And his clothes in the blood of grapes.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: His eyes are darker than wine, And his teeth whiter than milk.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Zebulun shall dwell by the haven of the sea; He shall become a haven for ships, And his border shall adjoin Sidon.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Issachar is a strong donkey, Lying down between two burdens;
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He saw that rest was good, And that the land was pleasant; He bowed his shoulder to bear a burden, And became a band of slaves.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan shall judge his people As one of the tribes of Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan shall be a serpent by the way, A viper by the path, That bites the horse's heels So that its rider shall fall backward.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I have waited for your salvation, O Lord!
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Gad, a troop shall tramp upon him, But he shall triumph at last.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Bread from Asher shall be rich, And he shall yield royal dainties.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Naphtali is a deer let loose; He uses beautiful words.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Joseph is a fruitful bough, A fruitful bough by a well; His branches run over the wall.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The archers have bitterly grieved him, Shot at him and hated him.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: But his bow remained in strength, And the arms of his hands were made strong By the hands of the Mighty God of Jacob (From there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Israel),
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: By the God of your father who will help you, And by the Almighty who will bless you With blessings of heaven above, Blessings of the deep that lies beneath, Blessings of the breasts and of the womb.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The blessings of your father Have excelled the blessings of my ancestors, Up to the utmost bound of the everlasting hills. They shall be on the head of Joseph, And on the crown of the head of him who was separate from his brothers.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf; In the morning he shall devour the prey, And at night he shall divide the spoil.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father spoke to them. And he blessed them; he blessed each one according to his own blessing.
Then he charged them and said to them:
I am to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hittite as a possession for a burial place.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife, there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife, and there I buried Leah.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The field and the cave that is there were purchased from the sons of Heth.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And when Jacob had finished commanding his sons, he drew his feet up into the bed and breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.
